summary: Dan Shapiro recommends Woodweb's forums as an online marketplace for finding lumber deals, from reclaimed antique timbers to exotic woods like snakewood at steep discounts. He notes that savings on rare wood can effectively pay for new tools. He advises caution due to occasional scams, suggesting buyers ask for references and buy locally when possible to avoid shipping costs and verify quality in person.
key_points: Woodweb's forums offer a wide range of lumber from reclaimed timbers to exotic species at below-market prices
Snakewood was available at roughly 1/5 the typical market price, illustrating the potential savings
Buyers should exercise caution by checking references and preferring local sellers to inspect goods firsthand
money_quotes: if I buy four bf of snakewood, I've just saved enough to buy a new drill press. Brilliant!
It's important to be careful since there are a few ripoff stories floating around.
key_insight: Online lumber forums can offer exotic woods at a fraction of retail price, but buyers should verify sellers and inspect goods before purchasing.
